hi!! i was curious if there’s a document compiling all the morimens world building?
world building in what sense? i have a document for awakeners, but otherwise i only have the timeline sheet
ahh i was thinking more like the actual backstory of the game if that makes sense? like dissolution, the different realms, etc etc
mmhm i think i can answer some of that rn

dissolution is a natural disaster with still unknown causes (assuming ur in arc 1). what we do know is it that it can straight up erase existences, so complete dissolution would make the subject as if they never existed
also like the different “factions” ig, like lightbearer church vs salv’s church, and also lightbearer w/ sculptor’s association
the realms are more of a thematic thing, which i cover in my Awakeners 101
tbh most of these can be found in game but maintenance is happening rn 😭
yeah i can answer it but for spoiler safety i must first ask you what is your main story progression
i’m at astral reign, where dore is introduced
arc 2, so i can explain most stuff then
then lantern church is a offshoot of the chantry of life, think of it like how some cults are offshoots of Christianity
the sculptors are an unrelated association founded by an ex-mythag student (Hebert). they're currently have a coopoerative relationship with the Lanternbearers, but theyre not aligned on everything
oh yeah i lowkey forgot how does albino play into everything? is he part of sculptors association
albino is part of the lantern church, as mentioned in the Cursed Binding SR wheel
OHH okok
Keeper spends A LOT of time in the mythag infirmary
They are also being treated by doll and gdoll, who are probably the greatest medical geniuses on the planet
and finally, there are at least a few times where they really should have been bedridden for years, but healed instantly due to plot reasons
Basically they get stabbed and beaten up a lot but never in a context that they cant recover from it after like a month

Honestly, some of them are kind of interesting lore-wise, though. Or in terms of character writing, at least

Like, the fact that Doresain outright enjoys making himself wait for things he wants because he thinks that acting on momentary impulses is a waste is relevant to how he treats the protagonist cannibalism-wise, for example

And the fact that Uvash, murderous as he may be, is actually really sweet (in his own way, lol, it does still involve gore before he can be steered towards cakes instead) when he likes someone, adds genuine depth to his character
Stuff like that

In terms of raw powers and ability to affect the world, the "strongest awakener" is a tie between thais and tawil

Other contenders:
Doresain (is a king with an army)
Miryam (is a cult leader with an army)
Hameln (unknown power ceiling, did some crazy feats in ch8)

Lotan is really old but not actually that "powerful" outside of a straight up swordfight (she gets owned by ||hameln|| in ch8)

By that logic, a chess nerd is stronger than a pro athlete
If they're good enough
im defining "power level" as "ability to affect the world"
It's "talent", it can be "power", but it isn't "strength"
Sorry, I'm a bit of a pedant, haha
ok in terms of "strength" Goliath is canonically the strongest
It's in his talent name
The Strongest
He is very strong!
have you ever seen anyone beat goliath in arm wrestling?
exactly
In general, though, I find power scaling conversations kind of pointless. Because in a good piece of media, it always kind of depends on the situation and how the power is used and all that
How you value the various aspects
its a fun thought experiment as long as you define your criteria
Eh, I think it's a bit boring, haha. But to each their own

i said that mine is "ability to affect the world"

so the benchmark would be something like "if you had a contest between 2 awakeners and challenged them to go do a specific thing, which one is more likely to succeed"
Yeah, that would depend on the thing, though, wouldn't it
it would
Is someone with the power to turn everyone's eyes green more powerful than someone who can level a castle with a gesture? Stuff like that
I think at that rate, it makes less sense to rate power overall and more to rate someone's ability to do a specific thing
Which isn't really powerscaling

I think its still a useful way to think about it because often one character is an order of magnitude more powerful than another

Like, miryam clearly would beat murphy at most objectives
so miryam is "more powerful"
it does break when you evaluate people who have hyper specific abilities, but those people are a pain to compare anyway
Yeah, but that basically just means you end up with a couple of really obvious decisions that you don't have to think about at all and then a big pile of "idk it depends"
average powerscaling discussion
Exactly, that's why I find them boring, haha

What happened guys?

how insane of an idea would it be to start making a doc or something that holds the entirety of the morimens script
like, all the dialogue and descriptions from the main story chapters, possibly including the event descriptions from dungeons, psyche deepdives, dreamscapes, special ops, awakener stories, etc?

like I know it would take a ton of work but I get frustrated by not being able to just Go To A Script and find the text I need as reference for a fic of something, and the urge to spend my free time making a script is like a siren call
if anyone else is interested I wouldn't mind collaborators though I am aware it does sound kind of insane of a project
its probably easier for you to ask the localizers for a copy
although idk if they would release it

Could anyone explain me the basic lore?
I always struggle to understand the lore in every game at the start it only comes later...

The year is AF 315.

Big problem: Dissolution. Black marks are appearing all over the world and making people turn into monsters. Also, it removes them from existence and everyone's memories.

Mythag University is researching dissolulu to try to stop it. You are a student at Mythag University who became a Keeper.

Awakeners are people with superpowers. Mythag has the ability to extract Silver from dead people to revive them as Awakeners. A Keeper's job is to link to Awakeners using the Silver Key and command them in battle.
note: not all awakeners are dead people, but dead people are the majority
and the year is AF315, not 317
